summary: Domain/product review accepts the Run Scan API slice. The implementation matches the M4 opportunity-generation contract: the default local mock path produces 10 scored opportunities in focused coverage, saves candidates/SERP/opportunities with score breakdowns and explanations, preserves new opportunity status semantics, carries risk into persisted summaries/raw analysis/kill criteria and scoring, avoids market-proof or outcome overclaims, distinguishes completed/partial_failed/failed, and stays within the MVP boundary.

tests_run.result: PASS: 8 focused scan/API tests passed, including deterministic default mock scan with 10 opportunities, partial_failed retention of successful opportunities, failed run semantics, sanitized errors, API validation, and response envelopes.

FAIL in this review environment: Next compiled successfully, then failed during page-data/static-export after repeated local @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc native package errors reporting the binary is not a valid Win32 application. This was not traced to a domain/product issue in the changed slice; QA/release should rerun build on a clean dependency install.

decisions: ACCEPT: Opportunity generation semantics follow the documented chain from active RadarTask through keyword expansion, mock SERP, SERP analysis, opportunity analysis, calculateOpportunityScore(), and persistence.
ACCEPT: The default scan path is local and deterministic with useMockSerp defaulting true, MockSerpProvider used by default, no default LLM client, and no network dependency in focused tests.
ACCEPT: The default mock scan coverage verifies 10 keyword candidates, 10 SERP successes, and 10 persisted opportunities when using the active task dailyLimit of 10.
ACCEPT: Saved opportunity semantics remain product-safe for M4: the orchestrator does not mark generated opportunities as saved/build_next/built, relying on the Prisma default status of new.

ACCEPT: Risk-aware saving is sufficient for this slice: risk level and notes are persisted in riskSummary/rawAnalysis, killCriteria is saved, and riskLevel is passed into code-owned scoring so high/excluded risk receives the documented penalty path.

ACCEPT: README and API language frame mock SERP as deterministic local/SERP-like output, not as real market proof; no revenue, ranking, compliance, medical, financial, or business-result promises were introduced.

ACCEPT: partial_failed means useful opportunities were saved plus at least one recoverable keyword/provider/agent failure; failed means no useful opportunities were saved or a fatal pre-run condition was hit.

ACCEPT: MVP boundary is preserved: no dashboard/list/detail UI, cron, auth/session, real SERP or LLM setup, env secret loading, schema migration, package dependency change, or MVP Spec agent was added.
risks: Build did not pass in this reviewer environment because of a local Next SWC native binary issue despite successful TypeScript/test coverage; this should be rechecked by QA/release before final merge.

High/excluded-risk opportunities can still be persisted as new records with risk penalties and do-not-build signals. Later ranking/list/detail UI must surface riskSummary/killCriteria and avoid presenting excluded items as normal top picks.

The API response returns compact generated opportunity summaries with scoreBreakdown/scoreExplanation; future opportunity views should expose the persisted risk summary and kill criteria before any build-next workflow.
requested_action: Review the accepted Run Scan API/orchestrator slice for security concerns around the mutating local POST route, sanitized error handling, dependency injection boundaries, no-secret/no-network defaults, and persistence behavior.
